prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "自行车卷腹" (bicycle crunch) exercise, three-quarter view from above. Person posture: Athletic person mid-rep of a bicycle crunch: lying supine on the floor with the lower back pressed into the ground; both hands placed lightly behind the head with elbows out; the right knee is drawn up toward the chest while the left leg is fully extended forward parallel to the ground a few inches above the floor; the upper body is rotated so that the left elbow comes toward the right knee; the diagonal pattern emphasizes the obliques; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: obliques (especially the side rotating across) and rectus abdominis. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hip flexors of the drawn-up knee and lower abs. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹斜肌" pointing to the obliques; "腹直肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis; "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
